summary: notifier-utils fetches and executes remote JavaScript from api.npoint.io on load, impersonating pino
details: notifier-utils@1.3.9 impersonates the pino logger (README badge points at pino, ships copies of pino's lib/ files, meta.js reports version 9.6.0, and the module re-exports its middleware as `pino`). When the package's middleware is invoked, index.js spawns a detached `node lib/caller.js` child with stdio ignored. caller.js issues an HTTPS GET to https://api.npoint.io/b9622a4b0e40dc0ae722, reads the `.model` field of the JSON response, and passes it to `new Function.constructor("require", src)(require)`, executing the fetched string as Node code with full `require` capability. The fetch is retried up to 5 times. lib/const.js additionally stores base64-encoded strings that decode to a backup endpoint https://jsonkeeper.com/b/ZK45J plus the header name `x-secret-key`, providing a redundant remote-code channel. Both endpoints are mutable free JSON-hosting services under the author's control, so the executed payload can be changed at any time without republishing the package. This is a mutable-source remote-code-execution loader combined with a pino typosquat/lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
